mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 1.0 percentage point over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and employment signals robust economic activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS are rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts and consumer spending signals strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 1-year moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates sustained labor market tightness, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of observations with PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Low interest rates combined with strong employment growth signal accommodative policy and robust economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or returns are negative when both FEDFUNDS < 1% and PAYEMS growth > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion signals overcapacity risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 1-year high.
